$comment: RETRACTABLE TEETH -- per-finding-class enforcement posture with non-retractable floors. Advisory panel (Fable + Codex 5.5 + Gemini 3.1 Pro) unanimously required the floors to live here, in the merge-gated contract (mirroring authority-key-registry.json), never in per-host operator env. The operator's off|warn|hard is the MASTER block switch, NOT a per-class posture layer. FORWARD-DECLARED: never_delete_human_refs has no scan emitter yet (git_discipline evaluates only write_footprint_violation + pipeline_branch_naming_drift today) -- its enforce floor is reserved for when an emitter lands; so no migrated gate hard-blocks by DEFAULT today. Posture rungs: observe < warn < enforce. A finding at `observe` is RECORDED as a note (never warns, never blocks); `warn` surfaces as a warning (never blocks); `enforce` MAY block, but ONLY under the operator's `PHASE_LOOP_CONSILIENCY_GATES=hard` master switch (`human_required` is NEVER set regardless of posture). Resolution: effective = clamp(min_posture, max_posture, layered(default_posture, per_repo_override)); a per-repo manifest override may RAISE any class freely and LOWER only to (never below) its `min_posture`. `invariant` classes protect humans or the audit/authority chain and carry a real floor; `advisory` classes are freely retractable to observe. Any per-repo downward move additionally emits the non-retractable `posture_retracted` note so de-fanging is always fleet-visible.

finding_classes.description: RESERVED/forward-declared: protects human authority over their own refs. No scan emitter exists yet (git_discipline emits only write_footprint_violation + pipeline_branch_naming_drift); the enforce floor takes effect once an emitter lands. Non-retractable (min == enforce); blocks only under operator hard mode.
A write outside the contract's git-discipline write-footprint allowlist. Floored at warn -- a repo may sharpen to enforce but never silence to observe.
A pipeline-family branch drifts from its required naming. Cosmetic -- freely retractable; migrates git_discipline's hardcoded note-not-warning posture.
A hash-checked document's committed digest no longer matches its recorded snapshot -- an audit-chain integrity signal. Floored at warn.

Repo-declared contract_version vs the installed package. The version-skew protocol's phase0 severity is a normative const `warn`, so this class is CAPPED at warn (max_posture) as well as freely retractable -- migrates the hardcoded capped_warn special case.
A spec-projection declares present-nonconforming. Loud by default but retractable -- conformance is a nudge, not a human/audit invariant.
A non-sanctioned presence-only spec-projection below the hash-checked bar. Info nudge -- migrates HGATE's hardcoded info tier.
A spec-projection sits BELOW its own contract-declared maturity_floor -- a real regression; advisory warn, raisable to enforce.
A spec-projection declares no maturity. Advisory warn.
A spec-projection declares an unrecognized maturity label. Advisory warn.
A spec-projection declares a governance-status label (foreign/unmanaged) -- not this repo's / ungoverned. Advisory warn.
A hash-checked doc has no recorded integrity snapshot yet. Advisory warn.
A hash-checked doc's committed blob could not be read. Advisory warn.

META: a per-repo override lowered some finding-class below its contract default. NON-RETRACTABLE (min == default == warn) so de-fanging is always fleet-visible -- a repo cannot retract the retraction notice itself. Carries the retracted class + actor/reason/timestamp metadata when present.
